macro_segments.title: Introduction and Review of Previous Material on Oath Violations and Korban Obligations

micro_segments.title: Opening Greeting and Daf Overview
Summary of Topics Covered: Shvuat Shav, Shvuat Sheker, Hatfasat Shvua, Konam, Achila, and Kezayit

macro_segments.title: Mishna on Single vs. Multiple Oaths: One Oath to Refrain from Eating vs. Separate Oaths for Eating and Drinking

micro_segments.title: Shvua Shelo Ochal (I Will Not Eat) Includes Drinking Despite Two Separate Acts
Shvua Shelo Ochal V'Shelo Eshteh Creates Two Distinct Oaths Requiring Two Korbanot
Analogy to Shabbat Melachot: Separate Transgressions Despite Single Underlying Prohibition

macro_segments.title: Mishna Cases: Bread Types and Liquid Types as Separate or Combined Oaths

micro_segments.title: Three Types of Bread (Wheat, Barley, Spelt) as Single Oath vs. Explicit Separate Oaths
Multiple Liquids as Single Oath vs. Explicit Wine, Oil, and Honey as Separate Oaths
Inedible and Unfit Foods or Drinks Exempt from Oath Violation; Non-Kosher Food Transgresses
Konam Conditional Oath: Wife Forbidden if I Eat; Effect of Eating Treif Food

macro_segments.title: Gemara: Drinking Included in Eating – Svara vs. Pasuk Proof

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Chiya bar Avin on Shtiyah Bichlal Achilah; Svara Explanation via Common Speech
Resh Lakish's Biblical Proof from Ma'aser Sheni: Eating Wine in the Pasuk
Rav Acha bar Yaakov's Alternative Pasuk on Shechar and Eating in Ma'aser Sheni
Conceptual Discussion: Power of Svara vs. Pasuk; Tosafot on Equivalent Strength of Logic and Verse

macro_segments.title: Gemara Debate: When Does Shtiyah (Drinking) Not Count as Achilah (Eating)?

micro_segments.title: Rava's Proof from Mishna: If Shtiyah Not Included in Achilah, Why Mention It?
Abaye's Challenge: If Drinking Included in Eating, Why Doesn't Second Oath (Not to Drink) Take Effect?
Rava's Response: Order Matters; First Oath Prevents Second Only If Drinking Already Included
Resolution: Context Defines Scope – Achilah as Broad vs. Narrow When Paired with Shtiyah

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Separate Oaths: Requirement for Alternative Phrasing to Combine Oaths into One

micro_segments.title: Why Repeat 'Bread Of' Instead of Simply Naming Grains? Gemara Explores Why Alternative Phrasing Matters
Principle: Separate Oaths Only if Alternative Grouped Phrasing Was Possible (Even if Lengthier)
Rav Papa's Context: Wine, Oil, Honey on Table; Could Have Said 'These Three Things' Instead
Rav Acha Bar Acha: Pessirah Context – Could Have Said 'I Won't Drink With You' Instead of Naming Each

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Oaths in Litigation Context: Multiple Oaths on Same Matter Create Multiple Korbanot

micro_segments.title: Mishna: 'Give Me Wheat, Barley, and Spelt' – One Oath vs. Three Separate Oaths
Rabbi Yochanan: Even Half-Pruta of Each Type Combines to Obligate One Korban if Global Oath
Rav Acha vs. Ravina Debate: Global Oath Plus Specifics (Four Oaths) vs. Only Specifics (Three Oaths)

macro_segments.title: Gemara on Oaths in Litigation Context: Multiple Oaths on Same Matter Create Multiple Korbanot (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rava's Application to Shechar Oath: Do Not Read as General Statement Else Specifics Don't Take Effect

macro_segments.title: Gemara Final Topic: Oath Not to Eat Includes Treif Food but Raises Question of Mushba V'Omed

micro_segments.title: Apparent Contradiction: Inedible Foods Exempt, but Treif Food (Non-Kosher but Edible) Obligates
Initial Answer: Distinction Between 'Stam' (General) Oath vs. 'Meforash' (Explicit) Oath on Treif
Mushba V'Omed Problem: How Does Oath Take Effect on Already Forbidden Treif If Already Prohibited from Sinai?
Rav, Shmuel, Rabbi Yochanan: Bundling Permitted with Prohibited Items Makes Oath Effective on Both
Reish Lakish Alternative: Oath Takes Effect on Treif Only if Specifying Less Than Kezayit or per Rabbi Akiva's View

topical_tags.term: chiruk melachot (distinction of labors on Shabbat)
ain shvuah chalah al shvuah (one oath does not take effect after another)
stam vs. meforash (general vs. explicit statement)
